summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a monster second set featuring St. Stephen, Eyes of the World, Lightnin' and Franklin's Tower
review: The reviewer praises this high‑quality soundboard source, noting crisp mix and removed dead‑air gaps. Musically the show is highlighted as one of the best versions ever heard, with standout performances of St. Stephen → Eyes of the World → Let It Grow, plus superb renditions of Lightnin', Supplication, High Time, Samson & Delilah, Dancing in the Street, Wharf Rat and a surprise encore of Franklin's Tower.

ai_rating.rationale: A single detailed 5‑star review emphasizes both superb musical performance and excellent soundboard quality, leading to a high rating despite limited sample size.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very high‑quality soundboard with great separation; dead air removed, clear mix, Phil's bass sits nicely in the front.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been The Roses
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set is a ‘monster list’ that flows seamlessly from St. Stephen into Eyes of the World, then into Let It Grow and a drum solo before moving through ballads and jam‑heavy tunes, ending with an unexpected but thrilling Franklin's Tower encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mesmerizing guitar work, expressive vocals and solid lead singing throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is very nice in the mix, big fat lines and lyrical licks are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides strong rhythm/lead guitar; occasional comment that he can’t keep up with Garcia at breakneck speed.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith appears but not as forward as in ’73/’74, still adds texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum solo transitions smoothly back into Let It Grow and is described as sweet.
